mnemonics.title: Wumpus Percepts
mnemonics.content: S-B-G-S: Stench (Wumpus), Breeze (Pit), Glitter (Gold), Scream (Wumpus dead). Remember: 'Stinky Beast Gets Scary'

acronyms.usage: Central storage of facts in knowledge-based agent
Logic with quantifiers and predicates for representation
Expresses that sentence α logically follows from KB
ASK queries KB, TELL adds new knowledge

visualTips.visualization: Draw 4x4 grid. Mark (1,1) as start. Show percepts in each cell. Agent sees stench at (1,2) and (2,1) - conclude Wumpus at (2,2) or (1,3) or (3,1). Draw arrows showing safe path.
Box diagram: Percepts → Sensors → TELL(KB) → Inference Engine ← ASK → Action → Actuators → Environment. Show feedback loop.
P → Q (arrow down), P (top), therefore Q (bottom). Show as implication chain: 'If P then Q, and P is true, so Q follows'.
Draw circles for domain. ∀ means 'all inside circle', ∃ means 'at least one in circle'. Show subset relationships.

analogies.analogy: Like a library or encyclopedia - stores structured facts that can be queried and reasoned with, rather than raw data.
Like a detective - takes known facts and logically deduces new information, connecting clues to form conclusions.
Like mathematical proof - if the premises (KB) are true, the conclusion must be true in all possible scenarios.
Like exploring a dark cave with limited information - you make safe decisions based on clues (percepts) and logical deduction.
Like following a recipe - start with ingredients (facts), apply steps (rules) to produce dishes (conclusions).
Like troubleshooting - start with the problem (goal), work backwards to find root cause (supporting facts).
